a:r.a:t: STAGE 4 - Ratchet Stage: Forward Secrecy & Compromise Isolation

A hash ratchet advances the key state forward after every message using a one-way function (SHA-256). It is computationally infeasible to reverse the function - you cannot derive K₀ from K₁. Each message uses a different, fresh key.
If an attacker captures the current key K₅, they cannot decrypt messages encrypted with K₁–K₄. Past keys are gone forever
the one-way hash chain cannot be reversed. This is the core guarantee of forward secrecy.
After detecting a compromise, a new ratchet chain can be initialized. Future messages are encrypted with completely new keys derived from fresh entropy. The compromised key chain is isolated
it cannot be used to decrypt new messages sent after the break point.
⚠ Attacker gets K₅ → cannot decrypt K₀–K₄
computationally infeasible to invert. This mathematical property is the foundation of forward secrecy.
